name: 2023-04-caviar
note: Caviar Private Pools (NFT AMM). Findings verified against the official C4 report (3 High / 17 Medium) and grounded to the clone's source lines. IDs carry the report's H-/M- number.

known_findings.summary: buy() computes royalties in a first loop, then refunds excess ETH to msg.sender via safeTransferETH, then re-reads _getRoyalty in a second loop to pay them. The refund is an untrusted external call between the two reads, so a malicious buyer's receive() can raise the NFT's royalty (e.g. 0% -> 100%) and drain the pool's base tokens to the royalty recipient (H-01). A TOCTOU/read-after-external-call accounting bug over external state, not a modeled sink.

execute() lets the owner make an arbitrary target.call{value:msg.value}(data); the owner can approve/transfer NFTs or tokens held by the pool (including users' in-flight or flash-loaned assets), an unrestricted privileged external-call sink (H-02).

buy()/sell() compute royalties using a single per-item sale price (salePrice = netAmount / tokenIds.length) even when NFTs carry different weights, so royalties for heavier/lighter NFTs are mis-paid and traders/royalty recipients lose their fair share (M-07/M-08). Protocol-specific fee accounting.

flashLoan's fee handling is inconsistent: flashFee() returns an unscaled value (unlike changeFeeQuote) and the fee is pulled from the wrong address / not routed to the factory, so the flash-loan fee is mis-accounted (M-03/M-06/M-16). Protocol-specific fee accounting.

EthRouter.sell pays each NFT's royalty via royaltyRecipient.safeTransferETH using the recipient/amount returned per NFT; a non-payable or reverting royalty recipient (or an inflated royalty leg) makes the sell fail or mis-pays the royalty (M-14). Protocol-specific external-data accounting issue.
